rationale: X's comment on reusing pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded component list echoes the spirit of dsocek's ground-truth concern about driving matching from existing per-pipeline metadata rather than ad-hoc lists, and adds a grounded, actionable follow-up about text-encoder LoRA silently no-op'ing. Y is purely a validation scan ('no finding reported' across all categories) that confirms the diff is correct but surfaces zero reviewer-style concerns or concrete suggestions, so it misses the substantive ground-truth thread entirely and offers minimal actionability despite being well-grounded in what little it does state.
